source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Subsea Well Leak
actions_taken: ROV closed SCSSV control valves SV1 and SV2
Performed leak test to ensure integrity
Well shut in with continuous pressure monitoring
event.summary: On January 6, 2023, at 13:00 hours, Shell Offshore Inc. experienced a pollution event at Mississippi Canyon 943 where subsea well PN003-ST2-BP1 released approximately 16.61 barrels of fluid mixture (hydraulic fluid, methanol, calcium bromide completion brine, and synthetic based oil) to the environment. The leak originated from loss of integrity in the Surface-Controlled Subsurface Safety Valve (SCSSV) control lines, with fluid being released through the High Pressure vent on the subsea tree. The leak was detected and stopped by closing control valves SV1 and SV2 via ROV. The well was secured with 2 barriers and remains shut in with continuous pressure monitoring.
recommendations: Tubing to be pulled and analyzed to identify specific causes of failure
New tubing to be re-run before bringing well back online
BSEE to engage with Shell's analysis to inform policy and procedure to prevent future reoccurrence
key_phrases: SCSSV control lines loss of integrity

hazards.name: Pressurized hydrocarbon and completion fluid system

threats.name: SCSSV control line loss of integrity
Inability to build pressure in SCSSV control lines

consequences.name: Environmental release of fluid to ocean

controls.name: Surface-Controlled Subsurface Safety Valve (SCSSV)
controls.barrier_role: Primary barrier to prevent uncontrolled flow
controls.lod_basis: Primary safety system for subsea well control

supporting_text: In attempting to open the Surface-Controlled Subsurface Safety Valve (SCSSV), there was an inability to build pressure in the SCSSV control lines
The pressure loss was from liquid being released to the environment through the High Pressure (HP) vent on the tree due to the SCSSV control lines loss of integrity

controls.name: SCSSV Control Valves SV1 and SV2
controls.barrier_role: Secondary isolation to stop leak path
controls.lod_basis: Manual isolation of leak path through HP vent

supporting_text: After the leak path was confirmed, the ROV closed the SCSSV control valves SV1 and SV2. Then visually confirmed the leak had stopped.
The SV1 and SV2 needle valves remain closed on the PN003 Tree to isolate leak path to environment and performed a leak test to ensure integrity

controls.name: Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) Inspection and Diagnostics
controls.barrier_role: Detection and response to subsea anomalies
controls.lod_basis: Real-time subsea monitoring and intervention capability

supporting_text: The environmental release was observed by an underwater Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) during diagnostics on the PN003 subsea tree

controls.name: Continuous Pressure Monitoring at Tubing/A-annulus Head
controls.lod_basis: Continuous monitoring to verify barrier status

supporting_text: The well remains shut in and the pressure is being continuously monitored

Pressure at the tubing/A-annulus head is being continuously monitored. The pressure remains stable above seawater hydrostatic and below pressure to balance reservoir pressure, indicating both barriers have integrity

controls.name: Fluid Loss Valve (FS2)
controls.barrier_role: Secondary barrier to prevent uncontrolled flow
controls.lod_basis: Secondary containment barrier for well security

supporting_text: The well is currently shut in and secure with 2 barriers, FS2 (fluid loss valve) and subsea tree

controls.name: National Response Center (NRC) Reporting
controls.barrier_role: Regulatory notification and environmental response coordination
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement for environmental incident reporting

supporting_text: The release was reported to the National Response Center (NRC)
There was a report submitted to the National Response Center (NRC) #1357058 on January 7, 2023

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
